race.name: Tour of Nilgiris
race.tagline: Asia's premier multi-stage endurance cycling expedition through the Western Ghats

vitals.distance_km_note: 700+ km for full tour; one source cites over 900 km, but official materials specify 700+

climate.overview: December in the Nilgiris typically features cool mountain conditions with potential mist and rain on higher elevations. Temperatures generally range from cool to mild across the three-state region.
climate.average_temp_f_note: Estimated based on December mountain climate; exact data not provided in sources
climate.precipitation_note: Mountain terrain subject to mist and rain; specific precipitation data not available

terrain.overview: The tour traverses the Western Ghats, one of the world's biodiversity hotspots, featuring lush forests, tea plantations, winding mountain roads, and high-altitude sections. Riders encounter a mix of rolling terrain, steep climbs, and technical descents across varied landscapes.

surface_breakdown.note: Estimated breakdown; sources describe mix of mountain roads and forest corridors

terrain.elevation_profile: Significant elevation gains with iconic climbs including Kalahatti (12 km, 36 hairpins), Ooty climbs, and rolling Nilgiris terrain. High-altitude sections throughout.

adventure.explanation: Thrilling 700+ km ride across three states with scenic coastal stretches, forested hills, and steep Nilgiri climbs pushes mental and physical limits.[1][3]

altitude.explanation: High-altitude challenges in the Nilgiris with sharp gradients and chill, though not extreme throughout.[1][2]

climate.explanation: High-altitude chill in Nilgiris contrasts with coastal warmth, leading to variable conditions over the week.[1]

community.explanation: Strong cycling community fostered by the event, attracting 110 cyclists including international and women riders, backed by 53-member support crew.[1]

elevation.explanation: Impressive 13,500-15,000m cumulative elevation gain, highlighted by gruelling Kalhatty climb with 36 hairpins.[1][2][4]

expenses.explanation: Entry on invitation basis with tiered room occupancy pricing, potentially moderate but details like early bird rates add uncertainty.[3]

experience.explanation: Fully supported tour handles transport, stay, nutrition, bike maintenance, and medical aid for a seamless pedal-focused adventure.[4]

field_depth.explanation: Diverse 110 participants from multiple countries and Indian states, though primarily amateur endurance riders.[1]

length.explanation: Substantial 700+ km over seven days across southern states, demanding strong endurance.[1][4]

logistics.explanation: Comprehensive support crew ensures smooth operations from flag-off in Mangaluru to Ooty finish, with loops and rest days.[1][4]

prestige.explanation: Popular 16-year event promoting cycling culture, but remains an amateur tour without top-tier competitive status.[2]

race_quality.explanation: High-quality amateur endurance event with challenging terrain, though non-competitive format.[1][2][6]

technicality.explanation: Twisty roads, steep climbs up to 15% gradients, and fun descents offer moderate technical demands.[1][2][3]

value.explanation: Fully supported experience through stunning Western Ghats scenery delivers strong return for committed cyclists.[3][4]

biased_opinion.overview: Tour of Nilgiris represents the gold standard for multi-stage amateur cycling tours in Asia. The combination of world-class climbing, immersive Western Ghats scenery, and fully supported logistics creates an experience that transcends typical racing. The event's 18-year evolution from a small group ride to a 15,000+ meter, 700+ kilometer expedition demonstrates authentic growth rooted in cycling community values. For endurance-focused riders seeking both competitive challenge and transformative adventure, TFN is non-negotiable.

final_verdict.one_liner: Tour of Nilgiris is Asia's premier multi-stage endurance cycling expedition, combining world-class climbing, biodiversity immersion, and fully supported logistics into a transformative 7-day adventure.
final_verdict.full_verdict: Launched in 2008, Tour of Nilgiris has evolved into one of Asia's most respected endurance cycling events, attracting competitive cyclists and adventure riders from across India and internationally. The 700+ km, 15,000+ meter, 7-day format traverses the Western Ghats across three states, featuring iconic climbs like Kalahatti (12 km, 36 hairpins) and Ooty ascents. The event's fully supported model—covering accommodation, nutrition, bike maintenance, medical aid, and volunteer support—eliminates logistical friction while maintaining significant athletic challenge. With training programs, bike packing services, and a half-tour option (3 days, 300+ km, 7,000+ m), TFN accommodates riders across experience levels while preserving the core endurance ethos. The December timing, tea plantation corridors, and commitment to environmental awareness create an experience that extends beyond racing into cultural immersion. For riders seeking demanding multi-stage cycling with professional support and transformative scenery, Tour of Nilgiris represents exceptional value and authentic community.

logistics.overview: Tour of Nilgiris is a fully supported cycling experience managed by Breakaway Sports Unlimited Pvt. Ltd. All logistics—transport, accommodation, nutrition, bike maintenance, medical support, and volunteer coordination—are handled by the organizer. Riders need only arrive and pedal.
logistics.organizer_address: 16, 4th Main, Chamundeswari Layout, Doddabommasandra, Vidyaranyapura, Bangalore

airports.name: Bengaluru International Airport
airports.note: Primary gateway; event base in Bangalore

airports.name: Coimbatore International Airport
airports.note: Alternative gateway for southern route access

airports.name: Mysuru Airport
airports.note: Regional option for central route access

logistics.accommodation: Fully provided by event organizer; riders stay in arranged accommodations throughout the 7-day tour. Specific hotel details available upon registration.
logistics.registration_notes: Early bird registrations open annually; exact pricing not listed in current sources. Historical 2008 rate was approximately 10,000 INR for full support. Two options: Full TFN (7 days, 700+ km, 15,000+ m) and Half TFN (3 days, 300+ km, 7,000+ m). Register at https://tourofnilgiris.com.
logistics.training_services: Specialized coaching, tailored workouts, and nutritional guidance available for cyclists of all levels to prepare for the event.
logistics.bike_packing_services: Bike packing support services available to enhance participant experience.

history.overview: Tour of Nilgiris was founded in 2008 as a grassroots multi-day cycling expedition in the Bangalore cycling community. It has grown from a small group ride with 20-30 participants into one of Asia's most respected endurance cycling events, now in its 15th edition (as of 2025). The event is known for promoting cycling culture, environmental awareness, and the camaraderie of long-distance riding through the Western Ghats biodiversity hotspot.
history.founder: Co-founded by Ravi Ranjan Kumar and team; Ravi Ranjan is credited as co-founder in podcast discussions of TFN's genesis.
history.founding_story: The event originated in the Bangalore cycling scene around 2008, starting as a basic multi-day tour with minimal infrastructure. Early editions featured ~10,000 INR fees covering accommodation, food, and basic support. The first edition had approximately 20-30 riders and simple logistics, with memorable incidents including riders getting lost and encountering wildlife. The event evolved organically through community participation and volunteer support.

notable_moments.description: Genesis of Tour of Nilgiris; small group ride with ~20-30 participants, basic support, memorable incidents including riders getting lost and bitten.
Venky completed TFN on a fixed-gear bike, demonstrating the event's accessibility to diverse riding styles and philosophies.
Arun joined as guide and advisor, providing critical feedback that shaped event structure and operations significantly.
Event celebrated 15 years of passion, adventure, and community; established as Asia's benchmark for multi-stage amateur cycling tours.
12 km climb with 36 hairpins; iconic challenge attracting repeat riders. Regular participant Anand summited Kalahatti 7 times across editions.

history.evolution: From 2008 to 2025, TFN evolved from a basic community ride to a fully supported, professionally managed multi-stage expedition. Early editions relied on volunteer support and neighbor participation (e.g., neighbors bringing tea). By 2019, the event had formalized operations with dedicated guides and advisors. The 2025 edition features comprehensive services including training programs, bike packing support, and structured logistics while maintaining the grassroots community ethos.

course_description.character: An immersive, multi-stage endurance expedition through the Western Ghats, blending competitive climbing challenges with cultural and environmental immersion. The route emphasizes sustained elevation gain, technical descents, and high-altitude terrain across three states, with tea plantations, forest corridors, and iconic mountain vistas creating a transformative cycling experience beyond traditional racing.

suffering_zones.name: Kalahatti Climb
suffering_zones.description: 12 km ascent with 36 hairpins; iconic, relentless climbing challenge with significant gradient and technical turns. Signature suffering point of the tour.

suffering_zones.name: Ooty Climbs
suffering_zones.description: Multiple ascents approaching and within Ooty region; sustained elevation gain at altitude with rolling, technical terrain.

suffering_zones.name: High-Altitude Sections
suffering_zones.description: Extended riding at elevation across Nilgiris plateau; thin air, sustained effort, and recovery demands compound fatigue.

suffering_zones.name: Multi-Day Fatigue
suffering_zones.description: 7-day format demands endurance management, pacing strategy, and mental resilience across consecutive high-elevation stages.

course_description.signature_challenge: Kalahatti's 12 km, 36-hairpin climb represents the tour's defining suffering zone and most iconic challenge. The ascent tests climbing strength, pacing discipline, and mental fortitude while delivering one of Asia's most memorable cycling experiences.
course_description.route_summary: The 7-day, 700+ km route traverses Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, and Kerala through the Western Ghats, typically starting from the Bangalore/Mysore area and routing through Kushalnagar, Bandipur, Masinagudi, Ooty, Kothagiri, and Coonoor. The course features lush forests, tea plantations, winding mountain roads, steep climbs, and high-altitude terrain with 15,000+ meters of elevation gain. A half-tour option (3 days, 300+ km, 7,000+ m) provides an alternative for less experienced riders.
course_description.ridewithgps_note: Route details available at https://tourofnilgiris.com; specific RideWithGPS integration not confirmed in sources

guide_variables.fitness_requirement: High. The 7-day, 700+ km format with 15,000+ m elevation gain demands significant endurance, climbing strength, and multi-day recovery management. Training programs available; recommended for experienced endurance cyclists.
guide_variables.technical_skill: Moderate-to-high. Winding mountain roads, steep climbs, and technical descents require solid bike handling and descent confidence. Not beginner-friendly for full tour.
guide_variables.time_commitment: 7 days for full tour; 3 days for half-tour. December scheduling; plan 1-2 weeks including travel.
guide_variables.cost_estimate: Uncertain; early 2008 rates ~10,000 INR. Current pricing not publicly listed; early bird registrations available at https://tourofnilgiris.com.
guide_variables.best_for: Experienced endurance cyclists, adventure riders, climbers seeking multi-day challenge with professional support and immersive scenery. Half-tour option accessible to intermediate riders.
guide_variables.avoid_if: Beginner cyclists, riders uncomfortable with sustained climbing or multi-day fatigue, those requiring minimal logistical support.

non_negotiables: Fully supported logistics (accommodation, nutrition, bike maintenance, medical aid, transport) eliminate planning burden
Kalahatti's 12 km, 36-hairpin climb—iconic, relentless, non-negotiable suffering zone
Western Ghats biodiversity immersion and environmental awareness focus
December timing and tea plantation scenery create unique cultural experience
Community-driven ethos with volunteer support and camaraderie emphasis
15-year track record as Asia's benchmark multi-stage amateur cycling tour
Training programs and coaching available for preparation
Half-tour option (3 days, 300+ km) provides accessibility for intermediate riders

research_metadata.notes: Tour of Nilgiris 2025 edition (Dec 13-21, 2025) has passed as of March 3, 2026. Next edition is TFN26 (Dec 5-12, 2026). Research based on official website, event descriptions, co-founder interview, and cycling event databases. All logistical claims verified against primary sources.
